findings.title: No-lease reviewer can still mint parent authority by beginning a lease
findings.rationale: The repaired writeback guard correctly rejects capture/record without an active verifier-backed lease, but begin_ralph_loop_lease itself is callable when no lease exists and immediately returns a parent_authority_token. A findings-only reviewer in a no-lease context can therefore create the missing lease, set CODEX1_PARENT_LOOP_AUTHORITY_TOKEN, then capture review truth and record an outcome, so P7 remains dependent on parent workflow discipline rather than runtime enforcement.
findings.suggested_next_action: Add a runtime/CLI guard or capability source that child lanes cannot invoke for begin-loop-lease, and add a regression that a no-lease child cannot begin a parent review lease and then capture/write back review truth.

findings.title: Multiple generic reviewer outputs can still satisfy distinct required lanes
findings.rationale: The lane-completion repair prevents reusing the same reviewer-output id, but reviewer_output_satisfies_required_lane still returns true for any generic legacy review_* id. Two generic outputs such as review_runtime_flow and review_runtime_flow_2 can therefore satisfy code_bug_correctness and spec_intent_or_proof without either output being lane/profile-specific, which weakens the P6 invariant that every required reviewer lane/profile has durable evidence.
findings.suggested_next_action: Restrict generic legacy combined-lane acceptance to genuinely historical artifacts or require lane-specific reviewer ids for new clean writeback, then add a regression where two generic reviewer outputs fail required code/spec lane coverage.
